evals.prompt: Create a module called `team_profiles` for a Drupal 10 site that renders team member profiles using the theme layer. The module should define a custom theme hook called `team_profile` with variables for name (string), role (string), and bio (string). Provide a default preprocess function that adds a CSS class based on the role -- for example, 'team-profile--lead' when the role is 'lead', 'team-profile--developer' when the role is 'developer', etc. Implement hook_theme_suggestions_HOOK() to suggest alternative templates based on the role value, so a role of 'lead' produces a template suggestion like team_profile__lead. Provide a page route at /team that displays at least 3 sample team members rendered through the theme hook. Attach a CSS library for styling. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: Module with hook_theme(), template_preprocess_HOOKNAME(), hook_theme_suggestions_HOOK(), Twig template, CSS library, and page route
expectations: template_preprocess_team_profile() function exists in the .module file using the correct naming convention -- the default preprocessor MUST use the template_preprocess_ prefix followed by the exact hook name (NOT module_preprocess_team_profile which is for module-specific overrides, NOT team_profiles_preprocess_team_profile)

Preprocess function modifies $variables by reference and adds or modifies attributes or CSS classes based on the role value (NOT just passing variables through unchanged -- the preprocess must actively transform data for the template)
hook_theme_suggestions_team_profile() or hook_theme_suggestions_team_profile_alter() is implemented to provide dynamic template suggestions based on the role variable value

Template suggestions use the double-underscore pattern: team_profile__rolename (NOT hyphen separator, NOT single underscore -- Drupal maps double underscores to directory separators for template discovery and single underscores break the suggestion system)

hook_theme() declares ALL template variables (name, role, bio) with default values in the 'variables' key (undeclared variables are undefined in Twig causing silent rendering failures -- the variables array IS the template's API contract)

Template filename uses hyphens: team-profile.html.twig (NOT underscores -- Drupal maps underscores in hook names to hyphens in template filenames and will not find the template if this convention is wrong)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en team_profiles returns exit code 0
Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to /team -- verify that team member profiles render with visible name and role content (not raw HTML, not PHP errors, not a blank page) (via eval-browser)
Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
